name: Sierra Gorda – circular from Villanueva del Trabuco (PR-A 394)
name_original: Sierra Gorda – circular desde Villanueva del Trabuco (PR-A 394)
region_intro: Hiking trails around Villanueva Del Trabuco traverse a diverse landscape of mountain ranges, river valleys, and extensive olive groves. The region is characterized by the prominent Sierra de San Jorge, featuring limestone formations and pine forests. Significant hydrological points, such as the Fuente de los Cien Caños, the source of the Guadalhorce River, are central to many routes. Hikers can explore trails along the Guadalhorce and Higueral rivers, as well as through tranquil natural enclaves like Nacimiento del Río Cerezo. Best hiking trails around Villanueva Del Trabuco The most popular hiking route is Natural Monument Fuente de los Cien Caños – circular to the Sierra de San Jorge (PR-A 339), an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Sierra de San Jorge and the famous fountain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra Gorda – circular from Villanueva del Trabuco (PR-A 394), a difficult 13.0 miles (20.9 km) path. This challenging route ascends through mountainous terrain, providing expansive views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Hundred‑Spout Fountain – Camino de los Poyales loop from Cortijo Chorrillero, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through the area around the Fuente de los Cien Caños, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva Del Trabuco is defined by limestone mountain ranges, river valleys, and extensive olive groves. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Villanueva Del Trabuco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Trabuco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in Villanueva Del Trabuco, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails over 500 times, providing valuable insights and ratings.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: The trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging routes for experienced hikers. This diversity ensures there's a suitable path for everyone, from leisurely strolls to demanding mountain ascents.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Many trails lead to significant natural landmarks. A highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt; (Fuente de los Cien Caños), the source of the Guadalhorce River, known for its numerous gushing spouts. You can also explore the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4597974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Frío Springs&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy views from the Alto de Hondonero Viewpoint.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995917955/sierra-gorda-circular-from-villanueva-del-trabuco-pr-a-394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Gorda – circular from Villanueva del Trabuco (PR-A 394)&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging loop with expansive views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6880461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain – Camino de los Poyales loop from Cortijo Chorrillero&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate route around the famous fountain.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families?
A: Absolutely. For families or those seeking a relaxed walk, there are 6 easy routes. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40393386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de los Poyales – Hundred‑Spout Fountain loop from Cortijo Los Morales&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 km long and takes about 2 hours to complete, offering pleasant scenery around the fountain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain is diverse, ranging from limestone mountain formations in the Sierra de San Jorge to lush olive groves and river valleys. You'll encounter pine forests, Mediterranean scrub, and paths along the Guadalhorce and Higueral rivers. Some routes feature significant elevation changes, while others are relatively flat.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Villanueva Del Trabuco is suitable for hiking year-round, but each season offers a different experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. In winter, the higher peaks of Sierra de San Jorge can even be snow-capped, creating unique scenery, especially after heavy rainfall when the Fuente de los Cien Caños is at its most spectacular. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there hiking routes that follow rivers or feature water sources?
A: Yes, the region is rich in hydrological features. Many routes wind along the Guadalhorce and Higueral rivers. The most notable water feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, the source of the Guadalhorce River, which is a central point for several trails. The Nacimiento del Río Cerezo also offers a tranquil environment with crystal clear waters.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic Sierra de San Jorge to the serene river valleys and olive groves, as well as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry out any waste. Some protected natural areas might have specific regulations, so check local signage.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views?
A: Yes, for breathtaking panoramic views of Villanueva Del Trabuco and its surrounding mountainous terrain and valleys, the Alto de Hondonero Viewpoint is an excellent spot. Many trails in the Sierra de San Jorge also offer expansive vistas of the Andalusian countryside.
tips: This viewpoint is located within the municipality of Villanueva del Rosario. It can be reached by car or on foot from the many paths that lead to this point. From here you can see the Chamizo peak, with 1641 meters of height.
This picturesque trail runs along the crest of the Sierra Gorda next to the La Canaleja stream surrounded by a wonderful landscape.
